title: An explosion of AI deepfakes is redefining American elections
description: Campaign ads featuring AI-generated clips and images once sounded like a laughable concept. Now they're everywhere, with attack ads that place candidates in a wide variety of compromising — and fictitious — situations.Why it matters: This largely unregulated practice is warping the unspoken norms of political campaigns and blurring the line between truth and fiction.Some campaigns voluntarily disclose this AI use, but it's not required. title: World Cup 2026: Tunisia sacks coach Lamouchi, appoints Renard after thrashing by Sweden
description: Tunisia responded to their 5-1 World Cup thrashing against Sweden by sacking coach Sabri Lamouchi and replacing him with Frenchman Hervé Renard, the federation announced on Monday.

title: Trump threatens 100% tariff on French wines unless digital tax dropped
description: US President Donald Trump has threatened to set a 100 percent tariff on French wine and champagne unless Paris removes a digital services tax on technology firms. France imposed in 2019 a three percent levy on the revenues earned by technology firms - including US giants like Facebook, Amazon, Apple and Google's parent Alphabet - within the country's borders.

title: North Korean hackers steal Balkan identities to freelance in Europe and US
description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, June 16: Papers focus on the historic Pechersk Lavra monastery in Kyiv that was set on fire by a missile barrage blamed on Russia. Next: a French-Vietnamese woman is appealing against the companies that supplied the deadly Agent Orange chemical during the Vietnam War. Also: an investigation shows that North Korean hackers are stealing Balkan identities. Finally, a cat interrupts the final scene of "Romeo and Juliet" in a t

title: ‘We won’t leave children in the cold’: Lebanese monastery opens doors to displaced
description: More than a million people have been displaced by Israeli airstrikes and evacuation orders across Lebanon, with many of them left living in tents or on roadsides. But amid the horrors of war there are also touching acts of solidarity. Our reporters Elena Volochine and Antonia Kerrigan spent a day in a monastery which has opened its doors to the displaced from across the south of the country.

title: Trump says Iran deal signed but details remain unclear
description: US President Donald Trump said on Monday a preliminary agreement to end the conflict had already been signed by the US and Iran digitally, ahead of a formal signing ceremony in Switzerland on Friday. However, details of the deal remain unclear, with Trump taking to social media to deny some elements of the agreement that have already been reported in the media, including the existence of a reconstruction fund.

title: G7 summit: A case of Zelenksy and Europe 'versus Trump'?
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday joined a summit of the G7 group of leading powers seeking to up pressure on Russia to end the war against his country. The summit will likely see Zelensky and European leaders join forces in attempt to persuade US President Donald Trump to increase support for Ukraine and up sanctions on Moscow, says France 24’s international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani.

title: World Cup: France face Senegal looking to avoid repeat of 2002 shock
description: France get their 2026 World Cup campaign underway Tuesday when they take on Senegal in New York. Les Bleus will be looking to avoid a repeat of their 2002 World Cup match against her same opponents which saw them suffer a 1-0 shock defeat.

title: Flag debate divides diaspora as Iran begin World Cup with New Zealand draw
description: Iran kicked off their World Cup with a tense 2-2 draw against New Zealand in Los Angeles. Overcoming several hurdles sparked by the US-Iran war, the team's participation and representation in the Group G matches has been at the heart of multiple controversies, among them is FIFA's ban on the use of Iran's pre-revolutionary flag, which has left the Iranian community in Los Angeles divided.

title: No toll but ‘maritime service fees’ for Strait of Hormuz?
description: Shipping remains virtually at a standstill through the Strait of Hormuz, according to tracking platforms. That's despite US President Donald Trump's claim that crossings were resuming under his deal to end the war with Iran. Meanwhile a row is emerging over Iran's plan to charge levies on shipping.

title: G7 allies scramble to put Ukraine back atop Trump's agenda
description: US allies at the Group of Seven summit worked Tuesday to push the war in Ukraine back up the agenda of President Donald Trump after more than four years of fighting sparked by Russia’s full-scale invasion. The Iran conflict has in recent weeks overshadowed the war in Ukraine that Russian President Vladimir Putin launched. Host President Emmanuel Macron of France said he’ll seek to persuade Trump to continue supporting Ukraine and increase pressur

title: 'A good feeling': How fans in Iran experienced World Cup opener
description: Iran twice came from behind to salvage a 2–2 draw against New Zealand in a World Cup opener overshadowed by geopolitical tensions, protests and months of conflict with the United States. In Tehran, the match was an opportunity for many to forget for a moment the turmoil in the country as they came together at watch parties to cheer on their team.

title: Trial begins for former Central African Republic leader Bozizé over war crimes
description: The UN-backed Special Criminal Court in the Central African Republic will on Tuesday, with foreign judges, begin the trial in absentia of former president François Bozizé over crimes against humanity. Bozizé, 79, who seized power in a 2003 coup before being overthrown 10 years later by rebels, has been living in exile in Guinea-Bissau since March 2023.

title: Iran held by New Zealand in World Cup opener overshadowed by politics

Officials said the agreement could unlock reconstruction funding, but key details remain to be negotiated. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

title: Swedish intelligence warns of increased Middle East war fallout
description:
STOCKHOLM: A top Swedish intelligence official on Thursday warned of increased threats against American and Israeli interests, as well as Iranian dissidents, in Sweden because of the war in the Middle East.
	The Scandinavian country’s security, which had already deteriorated after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, has worsened since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran on Saturday unleashed a new war, according to Fredrik Hallstrom, head of operations for

title: Israel decided to kill Khamenei in November, defense minister says
description:
JERUSALEM: Israel took the decision to kill Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in November and was planning to carry out the operation around six months later, Defense Minister Israel Katz said on Thursday.
	Khamenei was killed in the first hours of the US-Israeli air campaign that began on Saturday in the first assassination of a country’s top ruler by an airstrike.

title: Macron calls on Israel, Iran and Hezbollah to prevent Lebanon being embroiled in conflict
description:
LONDON: Emmanuel Macron on Thursday called on Israel and Iran not to embroil Lebanon in the conflict sweeping the Middle East.

	The French president made his plea as panic swept through Beirut after Israel ordered residents to evacuate the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital and three villages in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa region.

	Macron said he had drawn up a plan to end hostilities between Israel and the Iran-backed Lebanese militia Hezbol

title: Jordan’s king condemns Iranian attacks in region during talks with Syrian president
description:
LONDON: King Abdullah of Jordan and President Ahmad Al-Sharaa of Syria discussed on Thursday the dangerous developments in the region this week following the US and Israeli attacks against Iran that began on Saturday.

	During their telephone conversation, both leaders emphasized the need for dialogue to prevent further escalation of the regional crisis.

title: Kane to miss Bayern game against Gladbach with calf knock
description:
BERLIN: Harry Kane will miss Bayern Munich’s Bundesliga match against Borussia Moenchengladbach with a calf injury, coach Vincent Kompany said on Thursday.
	The England striker, who has been an ever-present for Bayern this season, should be fit in time for the Champions League last-16 first leg at Atalanta next Tuesday.
	“Harry Kane is out. He got a knock on his calf and hasn’t recovered yet,” Kompany said at a pre-match press conference ahead of

title: PSG prepare for Chelsea clash with Monaco rematch
description:
PARIS: Paris Saint-Germain warm up for their Champions League last-16 showdown with Chelsea by once again taking on Monaco on Friday, as coach Luis Enrique looked forward to “the best part of the season” starting.
	PSG have not managed to rediscover the exhilarating form that led them to Champions League glory for the first time in their history last season, but they come into March still on course to retain their French and European titles.

title: "Extremely dangerous": Republicans spark fury with apparent meddling in Dem primaries

description: A dozen Israeli company stands were shut down Monday at a major international defence and security exhibition held outside Paris, sparking outrage from authorities in Israel. Tensions have persisted in French-Israeli relations, with France recognising Palestinian statehood last year and two far-right Israeli ministers banned from entering the country in recent weeks. description: The short-term truce hasn’t been made public, and the long-term peace deal it is supposed to yield doesn’t exist yet, so there is much still unknown about any agreement between the adversaries.

title: Malaysia appoints new anti-graft chief
description:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia appointed on Saturday a former high court judge as the country’s new anti-graft czar to replace its outgoing chief who was dogged by allegations of abuse of power and illegal shareholding.
	The appointment of Abdul Halim Aman comes as Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s government faces increased scrutiny on promises made to tackle corruption in the Southeast Asian nation.

title: Chinese panda pair headed to US zoo
description:
BEIJING: Two giant pandas from China are headed to Atlanta on a new 10-year conservation deal.
	Pandas Ping Ping, a male, and Fu Shuang, a female, will live at Zoo Atlanta, China Wildlife Conservation Association said in a statement Friday.
	The organization signed the research agreement with the United States zoo last year, it said, noting the deal continues “the ‘panda bond’ shared by the people of both nations for more than 20 years.”

title: Despite Iran tensions, King Charles III will follow his mother’s lead in celebrating US-UK bonds
description:
LONDON: The challenge for King Charles III as he embarks on this week’s state visit to the US is, as always, to live up to his mother’s example.
	The late Queen Elizabeth II wowed Congress in 1991 with a speech that celebrated the shared democratic traditions of Britain and the United States, quoted Abraham Lincoln, Franklin D. Roosevelt and Ralph Waldo Emerson, and highlighted the deep bonds between the two nations.

title: Australia refuses repatriation help for citizens in Syria camp
description:
SYDNEY: Australia refused to help repatriate citizens related to suspected Daesh militants on Saturday, following a report that some Australian families are trying to return home from a camp in Syria.
	National broadcaster ABC said four Australian women and nine of their children and grandchildren had left the Roj camp in northeastern Syria on Friday, citing the camp director.
	Syrian forces were taking them to Damascus to help them fly back to A

title: Saudi Arabia’s events sector push on track despite disruptions, says industry veteran
description:
RIYADH: Saudi Arabia’s push to position itself as a global events hub remains firmly on track despite geopolitical disruptions, according to one of the leading figures in the industry.

	The US-Israel war with Iran has led to disruptions to many of the Kingdom’s flagship forums, with the Saudi Arabian Grand Prix 2026, the LEAP technology conference, and Automechanika Riyadh among those to be postponed.

title: Startup Wrap: MENA startups draw capital as AI bets continue to accelerate
description:

title: US military says it struck vessel in Eastern Pacific, killing two
description:

The post Russian barrages on Ukraines biggest cities kill at least 11, damage historic cathedral appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: The Morman Way on AI - Sponsored Content
description:
There is more than one type of religious approach to artificial intelligence. DZ Kalman talks with Elder Gerrit Gong about the Morman orientation to AI.
The post The Morman Way on AI appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Daily Briefing June 15 – Despite Iran deal, Israel determined to hold the fort in Lebanon
description:
Military correspondent Emanuel Fabian on how much territory the IDF is holding in southern Lebanon and a planned permanent base in Area A's Jenin
The post Daily Briefing June 15  Despite Iran deal, Israel determined to hold the fort in Lebanon appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: UK PM Starmer announces ban on social media for under-16s
description:
British premier says he's 'not prepared to compromise on the safety and happiness of our children'; kids will be barred from TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, X, but not WhatsApp, Signal
The post UK PM Starmer announces ban on social media for under-16s appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: As Trump heads to G7, world leaders welcome Iran deal, stress need for regional security
description:
Regional leaders focus on Mideast, as other nations offer help with clearing mines from the Strait of Hormuz, while insisting Tehran won't be permitted to obtain nuclear weapons
The post As Trump heads to G7, world leaders welcome Iran deal, stress need for regional security appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Report warns Israeli restrictions pushing Palestinian economy in West Bank to collapse
description:
Deprived of tax revenues and work permits, 'economic conditions necessary for any Palestinian future other than permanent subjugation' being lost, warns International Crisis Group
The post Report warns Israeli restrictions pushing Palestinian economy in West Bank to collapse appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: France picks MBDA-Safran combo to supply multiple rocket launcher
description: Paris snubbed competing offers from Thales and ArianeGroup, as well as from U.S. defense giant Lockheed Martin and South Korea's Hanwha Aerospace.

title: French army shows Leclerc tank with once-mocked anti-drone cage
description: Once derided as primitive, roof-mounted armor variants have been adopted by both Russian and Ukrainian forces as drone threats proliferate.

title: Ukraine’s defense AI chief predicts ‘new paradigm’ of warfare
description: “The system that possesses more data and better understands that data, proposes solutions — that system will gain the advantage over the other."

title: French wine at risk: Trump threatens 100% tariff over digital tax
description: US President Donald Trump told the New York Post on Monday he would slap a 100 percent tariff on French wine unless Paris removes its digital services tax targeting tech firms such as Amazon, Facebook, Apple and Alaphabet.

title: AI, drones and Ukrainian tech on show at Eurosatory defense fair
description: More than 2,500 exhibitors are taking part in the Eurosatory defense fair this week near Paris, one of the world's biggest weapon shows. James Andre, reporting from the show, says that AI, drones and weapons developed by Ukraine during its war with Russia are among the tech in the spotlight this year.

title: French fans flood New York ahead of world Cup opener
description: It is countdown time for the French national side's first World Cup match. Les Bleus are set to arrive in New York before facing Senegal on Tuesday. New York has been celebrating its NBA title after victory against the San Antonio Spurs. French fans who have already arrived in New York have been soaking up the festive atmosphere.

title: 'We're all about America': Trump's birthday marked with cage fighting on the White House lawn
description: US President Donald Trump hosted mixed martial ​arts fights on the White House lawn on his 8àth birthday on Sunday, an unprecedented spectacle highlighting his willingness to blend the pageantry of his office with his brutal brand of politics and his family’s business interests.

title: Kyiv monastery: 'Not inconceivable that is was deliberately targeted'
description: The Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery, a symbol of Ukrainian spiritual and cultural history, caught fire amid heavy Russian attacks on Ukraine overnight.  While is "not inconceivable that is was deliberately targeted", there did not appear to have been a direct missile hit on the buidling, reports France 24's Gulliver Cragg from the Ukrainian capital.

title: Historic monastery left in flames as Ukraine faces major Russian attacks
description: Four people were killed while the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery, a symbol of Ukrainian spiritual and cultural history, caught fire, in the heaviest Russian air attack on the Ukrainian capital in two weeks, authorities said on Monday.

title: G7 summit gets underway with spotlight on US-Iran deal
description: Allies from the G7 group of leading powers will meet on Monday at a summit in France, which gets underway hours after the US and Iran agreed a deal to end the Middle East war. France 24's international affairs editor Philip Turle reports from the summit.

title: 'Sudanese people have been fighting for freedom for decades': Director Hind Meddeb
description: The director of a film that shows how the people of Sudan have spent years fighting for their freedom has spoken of its power. Amid the civil war in Sudan that has killed tens of thousands since it broke out in 2023, a film made four years earlier during the 2019 uprising against the dictatorship has become something of a beacon for the Sudanese people. "Sudan, Remember Us" is being shown in Paris at a masterclass that discusses how difficult and

title: #Trump celebrates 80th birthday with #UFC fight at #WhiteHouse
description: To kick-off celebrations for the United States' 250th anniversary, the #Trump administration hosted a #UFC fight in the White House garden. The $60 million event, which coincided with the US president's 80th birthday, came a few hours after Trump and Iranian officials announced they had reached a preliminary agreement to end the #war.
